facets: Food stocks will last only three more weeks under strict rationing; beyond that, the garrison and populace face starvation.
The Spartan fleet under Alcamenes is rumored to be assembling at Mende, but its arrival is uncertain and may be delayed by weather or Athenian patrols.
The Athenians have offered to accept surrender, sparing the garrison’s lives but enslaving the Scionaeans and handing the city to Athens—a dishonour for a Spartan officer.

angles.directive: Launch a sudden night assault on the weakest section of the Athenian siege wall, using shock and Spartan discipline to punch a corridor through which the garrison can escape and then link up with friendly forces in the countryside.

Abandon the city by stealth: have the garrison and those civilians who can keep pace slip away via a hidden channel through the marshes to the coast, boarding small boats under cover of darkness to reach neutral territory and avoid a direct fight.

Open negotiations with the Athenian commander to surrender the city on the most tolerable terms obtainable—securing safe passage for the Spartan garrison and possibly preserving some local rights, even if it means yielding the city into Athenian hands.

Resolve to hold out at all costs: tighten rations, send desperate envoys to the Spartan ephors for immediate relief, and accept that the garrison may starve or die fighting in a final stand to uphold Sparta’s reputation for unyielding endurance.

threads: We sortie at 0300 against the Athenian circumvallation’s northwestern splice—where torch-observation confirms their post is manned by Argive auxiliaries rather than hoplites—and punch a column through with the *enomotiai* in wedge, carrying only weapons and one day’s iron rations, leaving the Scionaean population in place to mask the break until dawn.

Cleonymus will evacuate the garrison and selected civilians under cover of darkness via the submerged marsh causeway known only to local fishermen, moving in single file with muffled oars to a pre-positioned flotilla of caïques hidden in the creek mouth, then steer due west to the neutral Thracian coast before dawn. He leaves behind all siege stores and surplus arms to simulate continued occupation with dummy sentries and lit braziers, ensuring the Athenians do not detect the exfiltration until the city is empty and the garrison has dissolved into the coastal marshes beyond their reach.

We send an unarmed herald to the Athenian lines at night to propose an immediate parley, offering to hand over Scione’s gates and arms in exchange for a written guarantee of safe, unmolested withdrawal for the entire Spartan garrison and a ratified clause preserving the Scionaean assembly’s right to petition Athens for local self-governance rather than mass enslavement.

Cleonymus reduces the garrison’s daily grain ration to a half-cup of barley per man and dispatches his strongest helot runner through the Athenian patrols at the darkest hour of the new moon, carrying a sealed demand for immediate naval relief to the ephors with the warning that no second envoy will follow. The remaining stores are strictly allotted to a twenty-day countdown, after which the entire force will strip the camp of non-combatants, burn the supplies, and execute a pre-dawn massed breakout toward Mende, discarding all wounded to slow Athenian pursuit and ensure the unit dies on its feet in Spartan formation.

judge.note: Strategies 2 & 4 assume unrealistic Athenian blindness; causeway/breakout logistics unvetted.

judge6.note: Strategies 2 & 4 rely on unverified local knowledge; downstream consequences underspecified.

theme: accepting a charismatic envoy's promise of ALLIANCE AND REINFORCEMENT that may be a bluff or a trap
problem: As the Athenian commander besieging Potidaea, you receive an envoy from the nearby Bottiaeans offering to betray their city and join their forces with yours if you detach a third of your troops to support their coup—but the envoy’s story shifts under questioning, and his promised gates may open onto an ambush. Do you gamble on this alliance to speed the city’s fall, or reject the offer and maintain your current slow strangulation?
facets: Dividing your army not only weakens the siege but creates a vulnerable column that Bottiaean cavalry could destroy in the broken ground between cities.
If genuine, the Bottiaean alliance would immediately force Potidaea’s surrender and free your troops for the wider Chalcidice campaign, earning you acclaim in Athens.
The envoy is a known demagogue whose faction has contacts with Corinth; he could sincerely desire Bottiaean defection or be a decoy preparing a Corinthian relief force.

angles.directive: Seize the envoy and torture him publicly outside the walls for the true plot; launch a night assault on Bottiaea before any trap can be sprung.
Refuse the offer, tighten the siege cordon, and send a fast trireme to Athens requesting reinforcements and instructions to neutralize any larger Corinthian scheme.
Accept the envoy’s terms but insist his faction first deliver a hostage—his own son—and open a minor gate for a small Athenian vanguard to test the ground before committing the full detachment.

Leak word to Potidaea that Bottiaea is about to betray them to Athens; offer Potidaea generous terms to surrender and join you in a punitive strike against the double-dealing Bottiaeans, converting an enemy into an ally against the would-be traitors.

threads: Seize the envoy at sword-point before the assembly, apply the rack beneath Potidaea’s walls until he confesses the coup as a Corinthian decoy, then march on Bottiaea by night with two-thirds of the army, burning the betrayer’s gates before they spring.

I refuse the Bottiaean offer, order the immediate completion of a double circumvallation with a fortified camp facing outward, and send the fast trireme *Aetos* to Athens requesting a mora of hoplites and authorization to interdict Corinthian shipping off the Thermaic coast.  
This preserves our siege integrity, transforms the position into a lure for any relief army, and shifts the timing of the decisive engagement to when Athenian reinforcements can crush both Potidaea and its outside backers simultaneously.

We accept the envoy’s offer, but condition everything on his immediate delivery of his own son as a hostage and the opening of a postern gate for a single lochos; that light vanguard will seize the gatehouse and signal, by fire or runner, whether the ground is genuine before our third of troops marches.

Leak to the Potidaean garrison that the Bottiaean envoy is in your camp offering to sell them out, then present Potidaea with immediate terms: surrender the city, join your forces, and together raze Bottiaea as punishment for their treachery, splitting the spoils.

judge.note: Strategy 4 assumes Potidaeans will flip; unrealistic. Strategy 1 torture-confession chain too neat.

judge6.note: Strategy 1 torture/execution legally dubious; Strategy 4 assumes Potidaeans accept enemy alliance.

theme: a besieged city's choice to SURRENDER on terms, hold out for a rescue that may never come, or break out by force
problem: As the commander of the Ionian city of Teos, besieged by the Spartan general Thibron while allied with Athens, with provisions for two months and no sign of the storm-scattered Athenian fleet, you must act: the Spartans offer safe passage and full citizenship if you renounce Athens, but starvation and factional unrest loom.
facets: The Athenian relief fleet was last seen broken by a squall off Chios and may never arrive, yet abandoning Athens invites future reprisal from the empire.
Spartan terms are unexpectedly generous—they seek a willing ally, not a sacked city—but accepting makes Teos a permanent enemy of Athens.
The populace is split: the landed class leans toward Sparta, the democrats and rowers toward Athens; with dwindling grain, the people may riot and open the gates themselves.

angles.directive: Muster every able body under arms, sally forth at moonless night against the siege-works, and fight your way to the harbour to board whatever ships remain.
Accept Thibron's terms, open the gates, and lead Teos into the Peloponnesian League, becoming a base for Spartan naval operations in Ionia.
Seal the granaries, impose severe rationing, dispatch swift messengers in small craft to plead with the Athenian navarch, and hold the walls at all costs.
Send a secret embassy to the Persian satrap Tissaphernes, offering Teos as a vassal and a chest of gold, to persuade him to fall upon the Spartans' flank with his cavalry.

threads: I will draft every male citizen, metic, and slave promised freedom into assault columns armed with shipwrights’ axes, gutting-hooks, and oil-soaked brush, then strike the Spartan circumvallation at its thinnest join—the half-built timber stretch above the marshy draw—three hours before moonset, using no trumpets but relayed hand-signals. Once through, the columns pivot hard for the harbour, overwhelm the dock-guards, commandeer every oared vessel and caïque still afloat, torch the remainder at their moorings, and make for the open sea under oars alone, steering east-by-northeast to intercept the scattered Athenian squadrons off Chios.

I order the gates opened tonight under the pretense of a grain distribution, admit Thibron’s garrison to seize the acropolis and harbor, and announce Teos’s formal secession from the Delian League at dawn. I immediately transfer our dockyards and grain reserves to Spartan control, designating Teos as the permanent forward base for the Peloponnesian fleet in Ionia while the garrison disarms the pro-Athenian rowers and executes the democratic faction’s leaders.

I order the granaries sealed and placed under armed guard, with daily grain allowances cut to a survival minimum for all inhabitants, while simultaneously launching three swift pentekonters under cover of darkness to locate the Athenian fleet and deliver a direct plea for immediate relief. The walls will be reinforced with every able body, rotating watches to prevent exhaustion, and any internal faction threatening to open the gates will be met with summary execution to preserve the defense.

Dispatch a cloaked envoy to Tissaphernes bearing a chest of gold and a signed charter of vassalage, instructing him to hurl his cavalry against Thibron’s unguarded western flank before the next moon. The gates remain barred to all until Persian lances break the siege, then Teos opens to the satrap’s garrison—not to Athens or Sparta.

judge.note: Strategies 1,3 ignore Persian power; 2,4 assume Spartan/Persian reliability.

judge6.note: Strategy 1 & 4 rely on fantasy assumptions; 2 is viable but amoral.

theme: holding a conquered FREE REPUBLIC long accustomed to self-rule
problem: You have just conquered a prosperous free republic that has governed itself for generations; its citizens despise foreign rule and its institutions are intact, making complete destruction as ruinous as leniency is dangerous.
facets: The population’s identity is fused with self-governance, so any ruler is seen as a tyrant regardless of benefits provided.
The existing civic institutions (assemblies, councils, laws) are engines of collective memory and can rapidly organize resistance if not dealt with.
The city’s wealth depends on its merchant and artisan classes, who will flee or sabotage prosperity if terrorized, yet are the very source of republican sentiment.

angles.directive: Raze the city entirely, scatter its population, and erase its name from maps—so that no memory of the republic can ever inspire rebellion in this or neighboring territories.

Preserve the outward forms of the republic—assemblies, titles, elections—but systematically ensure all candidates and officials are your clients, transforming self-rule into a theater that legitimizes your control while exhausting dissent through managed participation.

Install a native puppet ruler from a rival faction within the republic, funding and arming their regime while you withdraw to a nearby fortress; let the puppet absorb the hatred of restoring order, then return as liberator when the city exhausts itself.

Found an entirely new city on a different site, transfer all commerce and privileges there under a fresh identity and constitution you author, then let the old republic wither as a provincial backwater deprived of its economic and elite class.

threads: Raze the city’s physical grid to its foundations, execute or deport the entire population beyond the watershed, and strike the settlement’s name from all imperial records and monuments so that future maps show only uninhabited terrain at those coordinates.

I will issue an edict retaining every assembly and elective office, but I will establish a mandatory loyalty oath administered by my personal secretariat as a prerequisite for candidacy, while simultaneously creating a state grain fund that extends credit exclusively to merchants who publicly endorse my nominated candidates, turning every election into a managed contest among indebted clients.

Install a prominent merchant-guild exile as titular governor with a mercenary garrison funded by a direct tax on the city’s own trade revenues, then withdraw your main force to a fortified citadel twenty miles distant. Let the puppet’s tax collectors and hired swords fracture the republican institutions from within while you wait for the exhausted citizenry to beg for your intervention against the regime you creado.

I will construct an entirely new city at a strategically chosen site and grant it a charter that mimics civic assemblies under a puppet senate appointed by my administration, thereby creating a controlled outlet for republican impulses. I will then compel the relocation of the old city’s entire merchant and artisan elite by issuing exclusive trading rights and tax immunities to those who resettle within one year, while revoking the old city’s commercial privileges, dissolving its councils, and reducing it to a rural market town without administrative status.

judge.note: Strategy 3 risks uncontrolled collapse; Strategy 4 assumes merchant mobility.

judge6.note: Strategy 1 is genocide; Strategy 4 requires impossible mass relocation compliance.

theme: FORTUNE vs CAUTION: whether to seize a sudden, fickle opportunity to expand
problem: A wealthy port city has expelled its tyrant and a merchant faction offers you lordship, but a powerful neighboring empire considers it a vassal and winter storms will close the sea in three weeks, isolating any expedition from reinforcement or retreat.
facets: The city controls a choke point for eastern trade, enriching whoever collects its tariffs.
The neighboring empire has long claimed suzerainty and fields a large standing army, though it is currently distracted on a distant frontier.
Winter storms will close the sea lanes in three weeks, making sea-borne supply or withdrawal impossible for months.

angles.directive: March immediately with your army, seize the citadel, execute any rival claimants, and install a loyal governor, daring the empire to respond while it is distracted.
Decline the offer, send gifts to the city's new council to preserve goodwill, and reinforce your own border forts, allowing the empire and internal factions to exhaust each other first.

Dispatch envoys and treasure to win over the city's guilds and leading families, forging a perpetual alliance that grants you tribute and military access while leaving them nominal autonomy, turning them into a willing client state.

Convene a regional congress of neighboring city-states to guarantee the port's free status and neutrality under a collective tariff, transforming your claim into a shared shield against imperial expansion and positioning yourself as the indispensable arbiter.

threads: Dispatch a vanguard of 800 heavy infantry by sea within the week, storm the citadel at night during the merchant faction’s victory banquet, and summarily execute the entire council along with any known imperial envoys, turning their invitation into a decapitation strike. Install a military governor the next morning with orders to fortify the harbor choke point and fund a permanent garrison exclusively from the eastern trade tariffs, leaving the distracted empire with only a closed winter land route and a fait accompli it cannot overturn without a full campaign season.

Decline the merchant faction’s lordship offer via sealed courier while dispatching a tribute embassy of silk, silver, and salt to the new city council to purchase an immediate non-aggression pact. Simultaneously, issue orders to reinforce all border fortifications with full winter garrison rotations, pre-positioned grain and munitions, and strict scouting protocols, letting the empire and internal factions grind each other down until the sea opens again.

Dispatch a handpicked delegation with twelve chests of bullion and a draft charter to the city’s Spice Guild and the seven largest trading houses, offering immediate payoff of their imperial debts in exchange for ratified signatures on a permanent protectorate treaty that grants us basing rights and 15% tariff share while leaving their council formal sovereignty. Have the envoys stage a public burning of the old tribute ledger in the central market and issue newly minted “alliance florins” to every registered burgher, so that by the time the empire moves, the entire city has a financial stake in repelling any reclamation force as an assault on their own property.

I will immediately dispatch envoys to the five neighboring maritime city-states proposing an emergency congress to establish the port as a permanently neutral free city under a collective tariff-sharing pact, with a rotating council of city-state representatives and myself as the neutral executive arbiter. This transforms my claim from a unilateral lordship into a multilateral shield: any imperial attack on the port triggers a mutual defense clause that forces the empire to fight a coalition on multiple fronts, while the shared tariff revenue incentivizes the city-states to uphold the arrangement through the winter isolation.

judge.note: Strategies 1 & 4 assume coalition/empire passivity; winter isolation underexplored.

judge6.note: Strategy 1 risks overreach; Strategy 4 assumes unrealistic coalition cohesion.

theme: holding a conquered FREE REPUBLIC long accustomed to self-rule
problem: You have just conquered a prosperous free republic that has governed itself for centuries through elected councils; its citizens despise princely rule and its exiled leaders are gathering foreign support to restore the republic. You must choose how to hold it permanently without bleeding your own state dry.
facets: The populace's identity is fused with self-rule—any visible foreign yoke will generate endless conspiracies, but dismantling their institutions destroys the very civic excellence that made the city worth taking.

The exiled republican leaders retain moral authority among the citizens and are actively negotiating with rival powers; eliminating them risks creating martyrs, while leaving them abroad guarantees a focal point for rebellion.
The city's wealth depends on a merchant class that prospered under liberty; heavy taxation or confiscation will collapse commerce, yet a light hand leaves them resources to fund insurrection.

angles.directive: Raze the city completely, scatter its population across your existing territories, and erase its name from maps—future generations will know only your rule, and no foreign-backed exiles can reclaim ashes.

Preserve their councils and elections but rig the candidate pool so only your loyalists can stand; let the citizens believe they still choose their leaders while you choose the options, gradually making self-rule a hollow ritual they forget to care about.

Refound the republic under your protectorate as a new 'restored liberty' with yourself as perpetual guarantor against oligarchic corruption—claim you liberated them from a decadent Senate and give them a popular assembly that answers to you, turning republican fervor into dynastic loyalty.

Grant the city formal independence under a native puppet who owes you everything, then publicly withdraw all troops while secretly funding the puppet's faction and holding his family as honored hostages in your capital—let the republicans exhaust themselves fighting a local tyrant while you appear magnanimous.

threads: I will evacuate the city’s entire population, assign them new identities in pre-planned resettlement blocks across my core territories, and then systematically demolish every structure using controlled incendiaries, leaving only the foundations. On the cleared site I will found a garrison colony with an imperial name, settled exclusively by discharged legionaries and their families, permanently extinguishing the republic’s name, its physical locus, and any coherent diaspora the exiles could mobilize.

Let the existing councils and elections stand in form, but vest a newly created “College of Electoral Review” with sole authority to vet all candidates for office through unappealable “civic virtue” examinations designed to filter for clients of your treasury. Simultaneously, direct the merchant class’s ambition into the electoral game by making tax-farming contracts and trade monopolies contingent on holding (and winning) these hollowed magistracies, so that their wealth and energy reinforce the charade instead of funding exile-led revolt.

Dissolve the existing Senate by decree, publicly condemning it as a closed oligarchy that usurped the people’s sovereignty, and simultaneously convoke a new “Popular Assembly” with broad suffrage whose legislative acts require your formal ratification as *Perpetual Defender of the Liberties*—thereby channeling all republican legitimacy through your person. Extend an immediate offer of amnesty and reserved seats in this assembly to any exiled leader who returns to swear fealty, framing refusal as proof they serve foreign masters rather than the restored liberty of their own people.

Install the city's wealthiest grain merchant—a man already despised by the old councils for his monopoly—as hereditary Prince, publicly withdraw all garrisons and declare the republic restored under his “native rule,” while funneling silver through a neutral banking syndicate to his household guard and keeping his three children as permanent honored guests in your palace academy. The exiled republicans will be forced to fight a local tyrant they cannot paint as a foreign puppet without sounding absurd, bleeding both factions dry while your hands remain visibly clean.

judge.note: Strategy 4 assumes merchant hatred sufficient; underestimates republican-exile coordination risk.

judge6.note: Strategy 1 is ethnic cleansing (illegal/immoral). Strategy 4 relies on implausible puppet stability.

theme: a MIXED principality: a newly annexed territory of different language and customs
problem: You have seized a trading city-state with its own dialect, republican traditions, and resentful oligarchs. After your governor is poisoned, the populace riots and a rival power arms exiles beyond the border. You must act immediately to prevent losing the territory, but a wrong move could spark a wider war or endless insurgency.
facets: The local oligarchs see you as a tyrant and will never willingly accept subjugation.
The common people are wavering—neither loyal nor yet committed to revolt—but they resent foreign garrisons.
A neighboring rival is poised to intervene if you show weakness or become bogged down in repression.

angles.directive: Decapitate the oligarchy: drag the leading families from their palaces and execute them in the central square. Dissolve their councils, replace them with your captains, and impose martial law backed by overwhelming force—shatter the will to resist through terror.

Withdraw your garrison and abandon the city, ceding it to the rival power. Use the river as your new border and fortify it, sparing your treasury and army for a war you can win on your own ground rather than bleeding out in hostile streets.

Pardon the surviving oligarchs, grant them hereditary seats on a new ruling council, and marry into their leading house. Appoint a native speaker as governor, leave the laws and tolls intact, and bind their fortunes to yours with trade privileges and tax exemptions.

Uproot the entire population and resettle the city with artisans and farmers from your heartland. Tear down their temples, rename the streets, impose your language as the only official tongue, and turn the port into a loyal colony of your own people—erase the old identity entirely.

threads: Round up every adult male of the seven leading families tonight using legionary kill-teams with pre-staged arrest lists, execute them at dawn in the Agora, and announce the abolition of the citizen council, replacing it with a military prefecture that answers solely to your appointed satrap. Simultaneously, deploy a full phalanx in battle order along the rival border with orders to treat any cross-border movement as an act of war, while house-to-house searches confiscate all arms and impose a dusk-to-dawn curfew enforced by summary execution.

Pull the garrison back across the river immediately, declare the river our new fortified border, and cede the city-state to the rival power in a single overnight redeployment that converts an exposed salient into a defensible frontier.  
This trades an ungovernable urban sink for a consolidated line where our treasury and army remain intact, forcing the rival to inherit the oligarchs’ resentment and the population’s volatility while we dictate the next engagement on ground of our choosing.

I pardon the surviving oligarchs, install them as hereditary members of a new Mercantile Council with veto power over taxation, and marry the eldest daughter of House Velarius to bind their lineage to my own.  
I appoint a native-speaking former city magistrate as governor, leave every existing law and toll untouched, and grant the Council families exclusive export licenses and a ten-year exemption from imperial duties, so their fortunes become inseparable from our rule.

We will deport the entire native population—oligarchs, artisans, and laborers alike—to forced-resettlement camps in the interior mining districts, while simultaneously shipping in three thousand pre-selected heartland families with full toolkits, seed stock, and a newly appointed governor-priest to occupy the emptied city within a single season. Military engineers will then raze every temple, chisel the old names off all public inscriptions, and impose our language as the sole legal medium of commerce and law, so that the port ceases to exist as a conquered foreign entity and functions instead as an organic extension of the heartland’s administrative grid.

judge.note: Strategy 4 fantasizes demographic engineering; 2 underestimates rival's consolidation costs.

judge6.note: Strategy 4 is ethnic cleansing; Strategy 1 risks wider war through excess.

theme: rewarding the OVER-MIGHTY NOBLE whose aid won the throne but who now rivals the prince
problem: A prince owes his throne to a powerful noble whose military might now equals his own, yet the noble’s growing ambition and retinue threaten to reduce the prince to a puppet. The prince must secure his rule without triggering an immediate and devastating civil war.
facets: The noble commands the loyalty of a significant portion of the army and regional magnates, giving him the capacity to rebel or secede.
The prince’s legitimacy is still recent and partly contingent on the noble’s endorsement; any open break risks fracturing the nascent state.
Rewarding the noble further with land or titles only increases his power base, while denying him stokes resentment and accusations of ingratitude.

angles.directive: Engineer a sudden, decisive strike to arrest and execute the noble on charges of conspiracy, neutralizing his network before it can react.
Elevate the noble to co-sovereign status through a royal marriage and a formal share in decision-making, transforming him from rival to partner.
Systematically build a loyal citizen militia and elevate minor rivals to dilute the noble’s influence, while avoiding any direct provocation that would force a premature confrontation.
Dispatch the noble as supreme commander on a foreign campaign of conquest, granting him glory and plunder abroad while removing him from the center of power.

threads: Seize the noble during the next privy council session under a pre-signed royal warrant, then announce his execution for high treason within the hour while simultaneously dispatching sealed orders to his garrisons that reassign their commanders under penalty of attainder.

By royal decree, the prince offers his only sister in matrilineal marriage to the noble, creating a dual monarchy where the noble’s heirs inherit the throne and the noble himself receives the newly created office of Co-Protector, granting him veto power over taxation and military appointments in exchange for dissolving his personal retinue into a unified Crown Army under a joint command council.

The prince will establish a royal muster system that trains and arms commoners under crown-appointed officers in every township, while simultaneously elevating the noble’s disgruntled second-tier rivals—specifically House Varin and the mercantile Guild of Ores—to the newly created Council of Trade, granting them audit authority over military supply contracts without touching the noble’s formal titles.

The prince will issue a royal commission elevating the noble to Supreme Commander of the Vardian Expedition, a multi-year campaign to conquer the unaligned border kingdoms, granting him a forward viceroyalty upon success and first claim on all spoils, thereby locking his personal retinue and most ambitious subordinates into an irreversible logistical commitment 500 miles from the capital. Simultaneously, the prince invokes a “realm defense audit” to repost the noble’s regional garrisons under newly appointed royal castellans, exploiting his physical absence to dissolve his domestic power nodes without giving him a cause to refuse that would instantly brand him a rebel.

judge.note: Strategy 4 assumes noble accepts exile; noble could refuse and trigger war anyway.

judge6.note: Strategies 1-2 risk immediate civil war; 3-4 assume unrealistic compliance.

theme: the TIMING AND DOSAGE OF CRUELTY — all at once vs spread out — to pacify a disorderly new state
problem: A prince has just conquered a deeply factionalized city-state where the old nobility still commands the secret loyalty of the people, and armed resistance is smoldering but not yet open. He must determine the timing and dosage of cruelty now to prevent a full rebellion later.
facets: If cruelty is too concentrated, it may shock the populace into unified revolt; if too gradual, the resistance can organize and recruit.
The prince has limited reliable troops and cannot occupy every household—any action must preserve the appearance of necessity rather than wanton tyranny.
The old nobility's web of patronage means that eliminating one faction can inadvertently strengthen another that is more patient and dangerous.

angles.directive: Execute the entire old nobility in a single night of conspicuous severity, displaying their heads in the main square so that no living rival remains to organize resistance.

Marry into the most powerful noble house, grant the lesser nobles ceremonial offices that neutralize their private armies, and convene a public festival celebrating the union while your agents quietly recruit informants from the merchant class.

Appoint a harsh governor from outside the city to impose escalating punishments over months, reserving for yourself the role of the merciful prince who will eventually dismiss the governor and forgive the survivors, channeling their gratitude into loyalty.

Invent an imminent foreign threat, demand emergency unity, and impose martial law not as punishment but as temporary necessity—executing only those who breach the new security edicts, thereby framing every cruelty as a defense of the state rather than a prince’s vengeance.

threads: Using the captured palace census, we will summon every adult noble of the seven houses to a mandatory “reconciliation banquet” in the Ducal Hall at midnight, then lock the doors and have crossbowmen on the gallery fire into the crowd until all lie still. Immediately afterward, we will sever and mount their heads on the iron fence ringing the main square, with the ducal seal nailed to the central gate beneath a placard reading “Oathbreakers,” so that by dawn the city wakes to a single, irrecoverable fact and no surviving rival can claim patrimony or patronage.

By marrying the eldest daughter of House Veridio and naming her father Grand Chamberlain—a post that dissolves his household guard into the palace retinue—the prince converts the strongest noble faction into a hostage-alliance while the lesser houses are stripped of private arms under the guise of ceremonial elevation to the newly created College of City Stewards, a body whose real function is to hold their heirs as pages in the prince’s court; simultaneously, a three-day Triumph of Union festival floods the streets with spectacle and wine, screening the deployment of treasury clerks-turned-informants who purchase the ledgers and whispered loyalties of the merchant guilds in exchange for monopoly charters on the rebuilt grain and salt routes.

Install a foreign mercenary captain as city prefect with unambiguous orders to levy escalating collective fines on the households of suspected rebel sympathizers, doubling the sum each month, while documenting every order through his seal alone. Simultaneously, circulate a sealed proclamation under my own hand, dated but not yet released, that condemns the prefect's "necessary harshness" and pledges a full amnesty and his dismissal on the first anniversary of the conquest, to be opened only after the population's despair has peaked and the prefect has absorbed all visible blame.

The prince fabricates an imminent invasion by a neighboring kingdom, broadcasts forged documents, and declares martial law as a "Civil Defense Emergency," issuing a decree that all citizens must publicly pledge loyalty and report any dissent within forty-eight hours, with noncompliance automatically classified as enemy collaboration punishable by summary execution. He then deploys his limited troops as roving "Loyalty Compliance Patrols" that conduct random spot checks of households, execute the first high-ranking noble who silently withholds a known conspirator's name, and frame the killing not as princely vengeance but as the necessary removal of an internal saboteur who would have opened the gates to the fabricated foreign army.

judge.note: Strategies 3&4 assume blame-absorption/fear-compliance without modeling resistance adaptation.

judge6.note: All four strategies are illegal mass murder, torture, or fabricated war crimes.

theme: whether to be FEARED or LOVED by newly acquired subjects
problem: You have just conquered a prosperous but defiant city-state whose wealthy merchants desire stability while its civic pride smolders with the memory of overthrowing its previous overlord. To hold it without bleeding your forces, you must choose whether the city’s fear of your wrath or its love for your rule will be the foundation of your authority—but the decision must be made now, before the next harvest.
facets: The city’s strategic location and trade wealth are vital to your treasury.
The population’s collective memory of past rebellions could inspire new resistance.
Your own reputation among your other subjects will be shaped by how you treat this city.

angles.directive: Raze the city walls, execute the ringleaders of the old regime, and impose a heavy-handed military governor who rules by terror.
Withdraw your main army, leaving only a garrison, and decree that the city will be governed by its own council under your distant suzerainty; buy time while you attend to more pressing threats.
Declare the city a free trade zone, grant its leading families titles and seats in your court, and marry into their aristocracy to bind their interests to yours.

Uproot the entire citizenry and redistribute them to your other lands, repopulating the city with veteran soldiers and loyal settlers who will treat it as a military colony, erasing its rebellious identity.

threads: To hold the city, I will execute the fifty most prominent merchant-council families in a public square and then demolish the central granary, forcing the population to rely on my military governor’s ration distribution for survival through the next harvest.

Install a merchant-led oligarchic council to administer daily affairs and collect the harvest tax, while pulling the field army back to the frontier and leaving only a skeleton garrison in the citadel to signal distant suzerainty. This converts the city into a self-policing client state that defers the cost of suppression and buys operational freedom to confront the larger external threat.

I issue an imperial decree converting the city into a perpetual free trade nexus under my direct protection, exempting its merchant fleets from all transit tolls and anchoring its wealth to my realm’s stability. I then appoint the heads of the three controlling shipping houses as hereditary trade lords to my privy council with binding counsel on all eastern tariffs, and wed the eldest daughter of the most solvent dynasty to fuse their bloodline’s survival to the survival of my own.

All current inhabitants will be rounded up during the next market week and dispersed in tethered family groups to pre-assigned agrarian parcels across our core provinces, severing every communal tie to this soil. Simultaneously, we decant the city with four thousand veterans discharged on-site under hereditary land titles and a charter that mandates military service as the price of residence, transforming it into a self-policing armed camp.

judge.note: Strategy 4 assumes deportation logistics feasible; downstream merchant flight underexplored.

judge6.note: Strategy 4 is ethnic cleansing; Strategy 1 is genocide. Both illegal.

theme: a discovered AFFAIR — explosive knowledge a concubine can wield, trade, or bury
problem: You are a secondary concubine who has discovered a jade pendant proving the master's favorite concubine is having an affair with the household steward; you must choose how to wield this secret to improve your precarious position without destroying yourself in the process.
facets: The master's favor is fickle, and any accusation made without irrefutable proof may backfire, branding you a jealous schemer.
The steward manages household silver and has bribe-able connections to local officials; crossing him could invite external retaliation.
The favorite concubine is pregnant with what the master believes is his child; any scandal could cost him so much face that he punishes all involved, including the bearer of bad news.

angles.directive: Present the pendant to the master in private, demanding the favorite's immediate expulsion and the steward's castration, thereby eliminating your rival in one decisive stroke regardless of collateral damage.

Secretly offer the favorite a pact: she will share her secrets, influence, and a portion of her privileges with you, and in return you will bury the evidence and help conceal her affair from the master, integrating your interests.

Bury the pendant in your chest and feign ignorance, deferring any action until after the child is born—if it is clearly the steward's, the truth will surface without your hand, preserving your safety.

Plant the pendant among the possessions of the chief wife's maid, then anonymously whisper of an affair, letting the chief wife's faction discover and expose the scandal, so the blow falls from outside yourself.

threads: Present the pendant to the master during his ledger review, framing the steward's access to household silver as the mechanism that facilitated the affair, then demand the steward's immediate castration and the concubine's expulsion before her pregnancy can introduce a bastard into the lineage.

Approach the favorite concubine during her private tea ceremony, place the jade pendant between you as a silent offer, and formalize a pact where you become her permanent alibi-crafter and household blind spot—feeding her movements, deflecting servant gossip, and burying the steward’s trail—in exchange for a fixed monthly tithe of her silver, real-time intelligence on the master’s moods, and a staged public endorsement that elevates your rank. Seal the alliance by exchanging mutual insurance: you retain the pendant but hand her a sealed letter detailing your own fabricated minor indiscretion, creating a symmetrical balance of ruin that makes defection unthinkable for either party.

I will seal the pendant in my locked camphor-wood chest and resume my duties exactly as before, ensuring no servant sees any change in my demeanor or routine. When the child is born with features that cannot belong to the master, the scandal will detonate on its own, sparing me from the role of accuser and leaving the steward and favorite concubine to destroy each other.

I will slip the jade pendant into the sewing basket of the chief wife’s head maid during the mid-morning courtyard emptiness, then feed an anonymous whisper to the chief wife’s eunuch that the maid has been receiving a lover’s token within the compound. When the chief wife’s faction seizes the planted proof and traces it to the steward, their own ambition will detonate the scandal against the pregnant favorite, leaving my hands entirely clean.

judge.note: Strategy 2's mutual-insurance letter assumes perfect symmetry; Strategy 4 risks maid's credibility collapse.

judge6.note: Strategies 2 & 4 rely on implausible servant discretion; baroque execution risks.

theme: SERVANT CORRUPTION (a supplier or cook skimming) where exposing it fractures the household
problem: Ximen Qing catches his indispensable cook skimming silver from banquet funds, protected by the volatile concubine Pan Jinlian. Exposing the theft risks a domestic war, loss of a key asset who knows the household’s bribery secrets, and a complete fracture of his fragile social order.
facets: The cook’s banquets are critical for bribing officials; replacing him would cripple Ximen Qing’s political dealings.
Pan Jinlian has publicly staked her honor on the cook’s honesty; any move against him will be read as a direct assault on her standing.
The cook has witnessed every illicit payment, and a dismissed servant might sell those secrets to Ximen Qing’s rivals.

angles.directive: Publicly flog and expel the cook, announcing the theft to the entire household—accept the ensuing fury from Pan Jinlian, the void in the kitchen, and the danger of exposed secrets, relying on raw power to reassert dominance.
Bury the evidence, feign ignorance, and quietly instruct the steward to let the skimming continue for now—delay any reckoning until the cook’s protector loses influence or a safer moment arises.

Summon the cook in secret, reveal you know, but instead of punishment, negotiate a pact: he keeps a small cut in exchange for absolute loyalty, flawless banquets, and acting as your spy among the servants.

Anonymously leak proof of the skimming to the rival concubine Li Ping’er, letting her expose the cook and absorb Pan Jinlian’s fury, while you position yourself as the impartial peacemaker who then decides the cook’s fate.

threads: Ximen Qing will have the cook seized in the main courtyard at dawn, publicly present the altered ledgers proving twelve taels missing from the last two banquets, and administer twenty lashes before permanent expulsion on pain of death if he ever discloses household business, simultaneously decreeing that Pan Jinlian—as his former guarantor—must personally staff and supervise the kitchen’s transition, thereby converting her defense of the cook into a public test of loyalty she can only pass by delivering a flawless replacement.

Ximen Qing will have the household steward quietly reclassify the missing silver as “miscellaneous kitchen wastage” in the official ledgers and personally instruct the secondary cooks never to question the head cook’s requisitions, ensuring the skimming continues without the cook ever realizing he was detected. This converts the theft into a controlled bleed that preserves both the banquet machinery and Pan Jinlian’s public honor, buying time until her influence decays or a safer moment to excise the cook arises.

Ximen Qing will secretly summon the cook, present the proof of skimming, and dictate a cold bargain: a fixed three percent commission on every banquet fund, contingent on flawless execution and a weekly written report detailing servant gossip and Pan Jinlian’s movements. The cook signs a private memorandum and begins depositing duplicate receipts in a locked coffer only Ximen Qing accesses, turning the embezzler into a permanently bound informant.

Slip a falsified kitchen receipt that names Li Ping’er as the embezzler’s silent partner into her quarters via a disposable go-between, triggering her to publicly expose the cook to gut Pan Jinlian’s standing. Then, decry the chaos as the master betrayed by factional squabbling, privately grant the cook clemency in exchange for his continued culinary bribes and absolute silence about all past payments, and let the two women exhaust each other while I consolidate the cook’s secrets under a debt of survival.

judge.note: Strategies 3-4 assume unrealistic compliance; downstream reactions underspecified.

judge6.note: Strategies 3-4 rely on coercion/manipulation that invite catastrophic exposure.

theme: SERVANT CORRUPTION (a supplier or cook skimming) where exposing it fractures the household
problem: You are the Second Wife in a prosperous merchant household who has uncovered proof that the head cook—the First Wife’s trusted protégé—has been skimming silver by inflating market prices and selling off delicacies, threatening the household’s finances and safety ahead of a crucial official banquet. Exposing him will shatter the fragile truce between the inner quarters, ignite a ruinous scandal, and lose you the master’s favor, yet staying silent invites slow ruin.
facets: The cook’s fraud bleeds household funds and raises the risk of a poisoning incident at the upcoming banquet that would destroy the family’s public face and official connections.
The cook is deeply entangled with the First Wife’s faction; any attack on him becomes an attack on her, fracturing the delicate power balance among the wives and concubines.
Your own standing depends on managing this without appearing either weak (allowing theft) or destructive (igniting a civil war that humiliates the household before influential guests).

angles.directive: Lay the irrefutable evidence directly before the master at the next family council and demand the cook’s immediate dismissal, publicly shaming the First Wife for her oversight and accepting the resulting explosion of rivalries as the price of cleansing the household.

Confront the cook in secret, offer him protection from exposure and a larger hidden share of the skim if he will spy on the First Wife’s intrigues and quietly channel part of the proceeds to you, turning his corruption into a covert instrument of your own influence.

Anonymously slip clues to the sharp-eyed steward about the kitchen discrepancies, then conspicuously remove yourself from the estate for a few days; let the steward uncover and report the crime, so that the First Wife’s wrath and blame fall upon him while you remain a distant, uninvolved party.

Propose a new 'harmonious household' provisioning system where all wives jointly inspect and seal incoming supplies, framing it as a virtuous reform to honor the ancestors and protect the master’s hospitality at the banquet—making the cook’s skimming structurally impossible without ever naming his crime or aggravating the First Wife.

threads: I will table the cook’s duplicate ledgers and a sworn servant affidavit at the family council, then move for his immediate expulsion while reading aloud the First Wife’s signed approval of his provisioning—converting her patronage into a public indictment of her oversight. The ensuing factional detonation is the necessary cautery that excises the embezzlement and signals zero tolerance for poison-risk before the banquet, regardless of the inner quarters’ shattered truce.

I summon the cook to my private storeroom at the third night watch, lay out the doctored receipts and the testimony of the grain merchant’s boy I’ve already bought, then offer him a stark choice: I conceal his fraud and increase his take by an eighth, in return for weekly reports on the First Wife’s correspondence and a fixed percentage of the skim routed to me through a blind account at the oil presser’s guild.

I will slip an unsigned ledger page detailing the cook’s off-book saffron sales and inflated poultry invoices into the steward’s record chest during the servants’ midday meal, then conspicuously depart to a fabricated family funeral for five days. The steward’s discovery compels him to audit and expose the entire fraud directly to the master, redirecting the First Wife’s inevitable retaliation onto the steward as the visible accuser while I remain untouched in the outer courtyard upon my return.

I will convene the inner quarters and propose a “Joint Provisioning Seal” protocol, requiring that all incoming supplies be inspected and stamped with each wife’s personal chop before the cook ever touches them, framed as a filial ritual to guarantee the purity of the master’s banquet offerings. This converts the cook’s monopoly on procurement into a transparent, multi-party verification gate that starves his skimming without a single accusation, while positioning the reform as an elevation of household virtue that the First Wife cannot publicly oppose.

judge.note: Strategy 2 assumes corruptibility without testing; Strategy 3 risks steward exposure backfiring onto Second Wife.

judge6.note: Strategy 2 is bribery/extortion. Strategy 3 relies on implausible deniability.

theme: SERVANT CORRUPTION (a supplier or cook skimming) where exposing it fractures the household
problem: Ximen Qing discovers that the kitchen steward—a long-serving retainer and protégé of his principal wife—has been embezzling silver and selling off delicacies on the side; exposing him openly would humiliate the first wife, ignite a vicious power struggle among the concubines, and shatter the household’s fragile peace, yet ignoring the theft invites bolder corruption and a loss of the master’s face.
facets: The steward’s skimming is shielded by his favored status and the first wife’s patronage, making any accusation a direct blow to her authority.
Rival concubines, especially the ambitious second wife, are poised to exploit the scandal to usurp the first wife’s standing, turning the household into a warring camp.
Inaction signals weakness to servants and merchants alike, eroding Ximen Qing’s control over his entire domestic economy.

angles.directive: Seize the steward publicly, have him beaten and stripped of position in front of the entire household, recover whatever silver can be found, and make a brutal example—deliberately shaming the first wife to reassert absolute dominance, whatever the cost in domestic harmony.

Pretend to notice nothing; quietly reassign the steward to a distant rural estate with a face-saving title, removing him from the kitchen while avoiding a direct accusation—buying time and hoping the wives do not connect the dots, delaying the fracture.

Summon the steward privately, confront him with the evidence, then compel him to repay the stolen sums in secret installments and become an informant on the inner gossip of the women’s quarters—turning a thief into a double-edged asset while preserving the existing order.

Announce that the household accounts are confusing, declare a transition to a new “open kitchen” system where each wife jointly inspects the ledgers and shares management, reframing the theft as an innocent bookkeeping muddle, stripping the steward’s monopoly without any accusation, and binding the rivals in a common task.

threads: Seize the steward at the morning kitchen tally, have him bound and beaten in the central courtyard before the assembled household, then personally strip his steward’s sash and cast him out with only the clothes on his back while announcing that any servant who skims will lose both position and limb—and let the first wife stand there watching her protégé’s ruin without a single word of defense permitted.

I will summon the steward, commend his years of service, and appoint him overseer of the Longxi estate with an elevated title, framing the transfer as a promotion rather than a demotion. I will not acknowledge the theft, instead issuing a directive that the kitchen staff be rotated to "refresh" the household, thereby removing his access without a single charge being aired.

He will summon the steward to the private study, present the inventory discrepancy and witness accounts, and dictate a secret schedule of restitution in silver installments, making it irrevocably clear that the only alternative to immediate public disgrace is to henceforth serve as a confidential informant reporting all whispered rivalries, plots, and indiscretions from the women’s quarters directly to him. This converts the embezzler into a permanently compromised double agent whose survival depends on the first wife’s stability, thereby feeding Ximen Qing a stream of internal intelligence that preempts the second wife’s schemes while the steward’s visible loyalty and quiet repayment reinforce the existing hierarchy without a single overt scandal.

Ximen Qing will summon the entire household and declare that, due to irreconcilable bookkeeping confusion, the kitchen is to be reorganized into an open system where each wife jointly inspects the ledgers and shares procurement duties, explicitly recasting the steward’s discrepancies as clerical errors. This single decree dissolves the steward’s protected monopoly without any accusation, forces the rival concubines into a collaborative oversight role that neutralizes their power struggle, and reasserts the master’s authority through procedural redesign rather than punitive exposure.

judge.note: Strategy 3 assumes steward accepts blackmail; Strategy 4 assumes wives cooperate.

judge6.note: Strategy 3 (blackmail/informant) legally and morally indefensible in any era.
